metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a grep-based proving command for each doc-content AC (e.g., `grep -q 'committed HEAD' docs/repo-snapshot-gates.md`, `grep -q '\bcd\b' examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md`) so every AC has evidence-producing verification.
findings.finding: ACs 1–8 (doc-content claims) have no concrete proving command; only AC 10 ('make lint passes') has a machine-executable check. An executor must rely on reading the files, leaving verification effort- and attention-dependent rather than evidence-producing.
findings.suggested_fix:
Append a 'Verification' section listing one grep per AC, e.g.:
```
grep -q 'committed HEAD' docs/repo-snapshot-gates.md
grep -q 'committed HEAD' docs/llm-framework.md
grep -qi 'cd into' CLAUDE.md
grep -qi 'cd into' examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md
grep -qi 'cd into' examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/SKILL.md
```
These anchor the content claims with word-bounded patterns rather than bare substrings.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the target reconciled phrasing for the cwd/branch decoupling sentence in `docs/repo-snapshot-gates.md` (e.g., 'The CLI close path resolves HEAD relative to the invoking checkout, so cwd/branch decoupling applies only to non-CLI callers that pass an explicit ref') so AC 2 is unambiguous and grep-verifiable.
findings.finding: AC 2 directs the executor to 'reconcile' the cwd/branch decoupling statement so it 'no longer reads as contradicting' the ref=HEAD path, but does not state what the reconciled text should say. The executor must infer the replacement text from context.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — item 2; Scope — docs/repo-snapshot-gates.md bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add a one-line reconciliation note to ADR-0005 clarifying that `ref=HEAD` on the CLI close path IS resolved relative to the invoking checkout (making the decoupling note inapplicable to that path), or explicitly document in the plan why the ADR note does not conflict and no update is needed.
findings.finding: ADR-0005 contains a 'cwd/branch decoupling' note that the plan acknowledges may contradict the corrected close-gate behavior, but explicitly defers reconciliation as 'optional' — leaving a potentially contradictory authoritative reference in place.
findings.location: Out of scope section: 'reconciling ADR-0005's decoupling note is optional'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a single sentence to ADR-0005's decoupling section, e.g.: 'Note: the CLI `transition … closed` path uses `ref=HEAD, fetch=False` and resolves HEAD relative to the invoking checkout; the decoupling described here applies to the MCP/programmatic path only.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan (Scope or AC 5) exactly where in CLAUDE.md the new 'work in a fresh worktree' / cd-into-worktree guidance should be inserted (e.g., after the 'Completion-verifier protocol' block, or as a new subsection of 'Gate protocols'), and acknowledge it is a new section rather than an edit to an existing one.
findings.finding: CLAUDE.md contains no existing 'work in a fresh worktree' section or any worktree guidance — a search for 'worktree' in CLAUDE.md yields zero matches. AC 5 says this guidance 'instructs the agent to cd into the worktree,' implying an edit to an existing section. Because the section does not exist, the implementer must CREATE it, but the plan gives no location (where in CLAUDE.md it goes, what heading to use) and does not acknowledge this is new content.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope bullet for CLAUDE.md, change 'the "work in a fresh worktree" guidance (add the cd-into-worktree instruction)' to 'add a new "Working in a worktree" subsection (under Gate protocols or after the Completion-verifier protocol block) that instructs the agent to cd into the created worktree and run all subsequent commands from inside it.'}

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale in the plan for the documentation-only approach — e.g., 'A code-level safeguard is not needed here because the `ref=HEAD` mechanism is already correct; the only variable is agent working directory, which documentation and instructions directly control.'
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for choosing the documentation-only approach over a code-level safeguard (e.g., a runtime warning in the close command when the invoking checkout's HEAD differs from what the agent might expect). The 'Why' section explains the problem but not why docs-only is the right intervention tier.
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Why' or 'What' section, add: 'A code change is not warranted — `ref="HEAD"` is already the correct behavior; the failure is purely a matter of which directory the agent runs the close command from, which documentation and operational instructions directly control.'

findings.finding: `CLAUDE.local.md` appears in the Scope section as a file to edit but has no corresponding Acceptance Criterion. An executing agent must decide independently whether to verify this file and what 'done' looks like for it.
findings.location: Scope section ('CLAUDE.local.md (local-only; not committed) — worktree guidance'); Acceptance Criteria (no AC for CLAUDE.local.md)
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: '`CLAUDE.local.md` worktree guidance instructs the agent to `cd` into the created worktree and run all subsequent commands from inside it (mirrors CLAUDE.md AC).' Or drop it from Scope with a note: 'CLAUDE.local.md is user-managed and outside repo scope; CLAUDE.md AC covers the equivalent committed guidance.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
